content:
AUDIENCE:  
The reader is a senior business executive with a demanding schedule and no technical or scientific background. They have a strong grasp of business strategy, management, and decision-making but are unfamiliar with the technical details of artificial intelligence or computer science. They care about practical outcomes, efficiency, and how technology can impact business operations or strategy. They would find jargon, complex explanations, or deep technical theory boring or confusing. A clear, concise, and metaphor-driven explanation with a focus on real-world relevance and simplicity would resonate best. A professional but accessible tone that respects their intelligence and time constraints is ideal.

===BEGIN===  
Large language models, like the one powering this text, work a bit like a very advanced predictive text system. Imagine an expert who has read billions of documents and can guess what words come next in any sentence, based on patterns they've seen before. When you ask a question or make a request, the model considers all the words you’ve typed and predicts the most likely next word, then the next, and so on—building a coherent response one word at a time. It doesn’t “understand” the text like a human but uses statistical patterns to generate meaningful, often surprisingly accurate answers. For business leaders, this means you can get quick, well-phrased summaries, ideas, or insights without needing to dive into the technical details yourself. The model is a tool that helps you communicate and make decisions faster by handling language in a smart, data-driven way.  
===END===
